Goals & Temperature Check

Help us understand your priorities so we can serve you better.

Desired Outcome

What is your primary goal for this case?

Full Deposit Refund (-- outstanding) Refund + Statutory Penalties (--) Maximum Recovery (--+) Negotiate Settlement

Get My Money Back Hold Landlord Accountable Both — Money + Accountability Fast Resolution

Active Disputes & Risk Assessment

Risk level reflects strength of each cause based on current evidence and legal analysis.

#Cause of ActionStatuteStrengthKey Issue
1 Breach of Contract Common Law STRONG Lease terms violated re: deposit return. -- deposit, only -- returned. Clear contractual obligation breached.
2 Violation of Civil Code §1950.5 CC §1950.5 STRONG 21-day rule violation, bad faith withholding. Potential 2x penalty. Core statutory claim.
3 Fraud (Concealment) CC §1709–1710 STRONG Credit Bureaus / Creditors concealed material facts about property condition and deposit deductions. MLS photos contradict claimed damages.
4 Constructive Trust & Equitable Lien Equity MODERATE Deposit funds wrongfully retained; equitable remedy to recover specific funds held by credit bureaus / creditors.
5 Conversion Common Law STRONG Credit Bureaus / Creditors wrongfully exercised dominion over Client’s credit report errors. Clear credit restoration claim.
6 Breach of Fiduciary Duty Common Law / CC MODERATE Fiduciary duties in handling client matters. Details pending.

Projected Timelines

Demurrer Hearing--
Service of Process--
Discovery PhaseExpected post-demurrer ruling (Q3 2026)
CMC / StatusTBD — likely after demurrer resolved
Settlement WindowOpen — pre-trial settlement possible at any time
Trial EstimateQ4 2026 – Q1 2027 (if no settlement)

Credit Evidence Assessment Advisor

Complete the intake sections below to receive your personalized dispute strategy assessment. I’ll analyze your derogatory items against the Five-Layer Dispute Strategy and recommend the optimal legal theories and dispute channels for each account.

FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions Upload Documents Credit Intake
Awaiting intake data...
DATA BREACH
Major Telecom Breach Exposes 73M Customer Records

Under FCRA §605B, consumers can place expedited fraud alerts and block compromised tradelines. File disputes with all three bureaus.

MAR 2025•HIRECAR INTEL
AUTO FINANCING
CFPB Targets Auto Lenders Over Inaccurate Reporting

Metro 2 reporting errors on repossessions exposed. Under FCRA §623, furnishers must correct inaccurate data within 30 days.

FEB 2025•REGULATORY WATCH
STUDENT LOANS
Servicer Errors Surge After Transfer Wave

Duplicate accounts and incorrect balances reported. FCRA disputes and CFPB complaints are your strongest tools.

MAR 2025•CONSUMER ALERT
CREDIT CARD DISPUTES
Banks Failing to Investigate Billing Disputes

FCBA violations rising. Under TILA §161, creditors ignoring timelines face statutory damages.

MAR 2025•LEGAL BRIEF
DATA BREACH
Healthcare Breach Affects 2.3M Patients

Stolen SSNs used for fraudulent accounts. Place fraud alerts and security freezes immediately.

JAN 2025•BREACH TRACKER
AUTO FINANCING
Wrongful Repo Reports: Challenge Under State Law

California CCRAA §1785 provides additional remedies. Dual jurisdiction claims multiply damages.

FEB 2025•CA LAW UPDATE

Personal Credit Intake 12

Due: Mar 20 5 SECTIONS ▼

Identity Verification

4Due: Mar 20REQUIRED▼
Accurate identity information is critical for credit report matching and dispute filing. The name must match exactly as reported on your credit file.
I confirm this is my full legal name exactly as it appears on my government-issued ID and credit reports.
I confirm this is my current residential address and matches the address on file with credit bureaus.

Derogatory Account Inventory

1Due: Mar 20CRITICAL▼
List every derogatory item appearing on your credit reports. Include collections, charge-offs, late payments, public records, and any account you believe is inaccurate, unverifiable, or not yours.
Creditor / Furnisher Account # (last 4) Account Type Status Balance Date Opened Reason Disputed
✕
+ Add Another Derogatory Item

Unauthorized Hard Inquiries

1Due: Mar 25▼
Hard inquiries require your written consent (FCRA §1681b). List any hard inquiries you did not authorize. Each will be challenged citing permissible purpose requirements.

Public Records

1Due: Mar 25▼

Credit History Context

5Due: Mar 25▼

Dispute Strategy Assessment

Derogatory Items × Dispute Layer Matrix

Example — 3 items
EXAMPLE RESULTS — SAMPLE DATA
CreditorStatusLayer 1Layer 2Layer 3Layer 4Layer 5
Capital One
Credit Card		 Charge Off DEPLOY MONITOR PRIMARY SUPP. INCLUDE
Midland Credit
Collections		 Collections PRIMARY DEPLOY DEPLOY SUPP. INCLUDE
Synchrony Bank
Retail Card		 Late 90 Days N/A MONITOR PRIMARY SUPP. INCLUDE

Enter your derogatory items above and run the assessment to generate your personalized matrix.

Key Findings & Strategic Notes

CFPB Complaint Channel Strategy

Web submissions to Equifax yield the highest relief rates. Experian web submissions are critically low — certified mail to Fraud & Identity Theft unit is preferred routing.

Metro 2 Field Priorities

Balance / Past Due / Charge-off fields are the strongest dispute targets (objective, verifiable errors). Date of First Delinquency re-aging is per se inaccuracy. Compliance Condition Code (XB/XC/XH) is newest litigation area.

California Dual Jurisdiction

For CA consumers, CCRAA §1785.25 parallels federal FCRA §611 + §623 but adds state-level damages exposure. Always include Layer 5 for California disputes.

FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Five-Layer Dispute Strategy?▼
The Five-Layer Dispute Strategy is a comprehensive credit repair framework that attacks inaccurate reporting from multiple legal angles simultaneously. It combines FCRA Section 611 direct disputes, FDCPA validation demands, state consumer protection statutes, CFPB complaint escalation, and CA State Law overlays (CCRAA §1785) to maximize pressure on furnishers and bureaus to correct or remove derogatory items.
How long does the credit dispute process take?▼
Under the FCRA, credit bureaus have 30 days (or 45 days in certain circumstances) to investigate and respond to a dispute. A full multi-layer strategy typically spans 60–120 days as different layers are deployed strategically over time. Complex cases involving legal filings may take longer depending on court schedules.
What documents do I need to provide?▼
At minimum, upload your credit reports from all three bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion), a government-issued ID, and proof of address. For stronger cases, include any correspondence with creditors, payment receipts, and relevant legal documents such as police reports or FTC Identity Theft Reports.
What is EQFX SWEEP automated extraction?▼
EQFX SWEEP is an automated system that parses your uploaded credit reports (HTML or PDF format) and extracts derogatory items, account details, balances, and reporting dates. This data pre-populates your Derogatory Account Inventory and maps each item to the most effective dispute layers.
What happens after I submit my credit evidence assessment?▼
The CreditWithKen team reviews your intake data and generates a personalized dispute strategy. You receive layer-by-layer recommendations for each derogatory item, including applicable legal theories, recommended dispute channels, and estimated timelines. Court filings and notarized documents are prepared by the HireCar legal team as needed.
Can I track the status of my disputes?▼
Yes. The Documents section tracks all filings, court notices, and correspondence. Each document is assigned a unique control number (HC-YYYY-CAT-NNN) and shows its source. The analytics dashboard provides real-time metrics on your dispute progress.
What is the difference between HireCar and CreditWithKen?▼
CreditWithKen is the service owner that manages your credit repair engagement, intake process, and client relationship. HireCar is the legal operations platform that handles court filings, document control, notarized dispute preparation, and case management. Both work together to deliver comprehensive credit restoration.
Is my information secure?▼
All data transmitted through this portal is encrypted. Documents are stored securely with access restricted to authorized team members. Your credit reports and personal information are handled in compliance with applicable privacy regulations.

Landlord-Tenant Dispute Evidence (18 questions)

10 CONFIRMED 5 EXPOSED ▼

Deposit Timeline & Disposition

2 CONFIRMED▼
CONFIRMEDQ1: Was the disposition statement received within 21 days?
CONFIRMEDQ2: Did the disposition include an itemized list?
CONFIRMEDQ3: Deposit & Move-Out Details

Receipts & Cost Documentation

2 EXPOSED1 CONFIRMED▼
EXPOSEDQ4: Were actual receipts attached?
EXPOSEDQ5: Were vendor names and license numbers listed?
CONFIRMEDQ6: Were deduction amounts estimated?

Property Condition / Sale / Broker / Demands / Notice / Financial

7 CONFIRMED3 EXPOSED2 PARTIAL▼
CONFIRMEDQ7–Q18: Remaining landlord-tenant evidence items
10 confirmed, 5 exposed, 3 partial across property condition, sale & escrow, broker trust account, demands, and financial impact sections.

Upload and manage credit reports, dispute letters, funding documents, and correspondence with bureaus and lenders. All files are saved locally and can be retrieved when you return.

Funding & Lending — Capital Partners

Funding applications, approval letters, loan documents, and lender correspondence. Upload pre-approvals, term sheets, and closing documents here.

General account communications, income verification documents, identity documents, and program updates. Upload pay stubs, tax returns, and ID documents here.

CLIENT WORK PRODUCT — PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL Credit Restoration — CR-0000-0000

Credit Repair Action Plan

Prepared by SeedXchange, LLC — Document Research & Preparation Division
Next Review Date: --  |  Dispute Division  |  Credit Bureau Disputes

Executive Summary

Defense counsel -- filed a demurrer on behalf of -- and -- in --. The demurrer likely challenges the sufficiency of one or more active disputes in the verified complaint. New Client, as a credit repair client, must file an opposition brief and be prepared to argue at the -- hearing.

Key Objective: Defeat the demurrer on all 6 active disputes, or at minimum preserve the strongest claims (Breach of Contract, CC §1950.5, Fraud, Conversion) and seek leave to amend any claims the court finds deficient.

Response Timeline & Deadlines

Demurrer Filed-- (--, Defense Counsel)
Opposition Due9 court days before hearing (approx. June 23, 2026)
Reply Brief Due5 court days before hearing (approx. June 27, 2026)
Next Review Date-- — Dispute Division
Client StatusActive client with SeedXchange support

Cause-by-Cause Defense Against Demurrer

For each cause of action, the demurrer likely argues failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action (CCP §430.10(e)). Below is the opposition strategy for each.

#Cause of ActionLikely Demurrer ArgumentOpposition StrategyStrength
1 Breach of Contract Lease terms not specifically pled; no privity with all credit bureaus / creditors Cite lease agreement (--), deposit receipt (--), partial refund (-- on 1/19/25). All credit bureaus / creditors acted as agents per ¶7 of complaint. Attach lease as exhibit. STRONG
2 CC §1950.5 Violation Statutory requirements not met; itemized deductions may have been provided 21-day rule violation is per se bad faith. Only -- returned with no itemized statement. Demand 2x penalty (--) per §1950.5(l). This is the strongest claim — clear statutory violation. STRONG
3 Fraud (Concealment) Insufficient specificity; no duty to disclose; no reliance pled Credit Bureaus / Creditors concealed Client’s dispute claim from escrow/buyer during -- sale. Property sold for --+ while disputed items outstanding. Concealment of material facts during credit reporting process creates duty. Cite Bigler-Engler v. Breg for concealment elements. STRONG
4 Constructive Trust & Equitable Lien Equitable remedy not properly pled; no specific property identified Sale proceeds from -- transaction are identifiable. Credit Bureaus / Creditors were unjustly enriched by retaining deposit while profiting from sale. Request court trace funds. May need to amend for more specificity on proceeds tracing. MODERATE
5 Conversion Deposit was not specific, identifiable property; landlord had right to deduct -- deposit is identifiable personal property held in trust per §1950.5. Credit Bureaus / Creditors exercised unauthorized dominion by using funds for cosmetic renovations to enhance sale value (¶13). No lawful deductions documented. STRONG
6 Breach of Fiduciary Duty No fiduciary relationship existed with all credit bureaus / creditors; -- unlicensed Fiduciary duty analysis pending case assignment. Details will be populated based on specific respondent relationships. MODERATE

Key Evidence to Compile for Opposition

Documents Needed

  • Original lease agreement (--)
  • Security deposit receipt (--)
  • Partial refund evidence (-- — Jan 19, 2025)
  • All demand letters sent to credit bureaus / creditors
  • MLS listing photos vs. claimed damage evidence
  • Property sale records (-- — --K+)
  • Escrow closing documents
  • DRE complaint filing confirmation (if applicable)
  • All certified mail receipts

Legal Authority to Cite

  • California Civil Code §1950.5 (deposit violations)
  • CCP §430.10(e) (demurrer standard)
  • Relevant case precedent (to be determined)
  • Smith v. Park Place Apartments (emotional distress)
  • Bates v. Landlord (punitive damages)
  • Bigler-Engler v. Breg (fraud concealment elements)
  • B&P Code §10131 et seq. (unlicensed activity)
  • CCP §452 (judicial notice)
  • Blank v. Kirwan (demurrer standard of review)

Action Items — Demurrer Opposition Checklist

Contingency & Settlement Strategy

If Demurrer Overruled

  • Proceed to discovery phase
  • Propound interrogatories & document requests
  • Depose key respondents
  • Subpoena escrow records
  • Negotiate from position of strength

If Demurrer Sustained with Leave

  • File First Amended Complaint within 30 days
  • Add specificity to challenged causes
  • Strengthen fraud allegations with MLS evidence
  • Detail fiduciary relationship more precisely
  • Core claims (Contract, §1950.5) unlikely affected

If Demurrer Sustained without Leave

  • Appeal if core claims dismissed (unlikely)
  • Proceed on remaining active disputes
  • Focus on CC §1950.5 & Breach of Contract
  • Consider adding new parties via Doe amendments
  • Evaluate settlement at reduced posture

Damages Breakdown (Per Verified Complaint)

CategoryAmountBasis
Actual Damages (Deposit Balance)---- deposit minus -- partial refund (Jan 19, 2025)
Statutory Penalties (CC §1950.5(l))--2x deposit amount for bad faith withholding
General Damages (Hardship/Distress)--Financial hardship and emotional distress
Punitive Damages--Willful misconduct, fraud, concealment
Constructive TrustTBDOver relevant sale proceeds (--K+ property sale)
Fees & CostsTBDReasonable attorney/preparation fees + costs of suit
InterestTBDPrejudgment and postjudgment interest
TOTAL SOUGHT--+Per verified complaint prayer for relief

SeedXchange Fairness Allocation Agreement (FAA) Impact

Agreement50/50 split on successful settlement or judgment
Service Fee-- total (-- down, -- balance)
Low Outcome (Settlement)-- → Client’s share: --
Mid Outcome (Statutory + Distress)-- → Client’s share: --
High Outcome (Full Recovery)-- → Client’s share: --
Maximum (Per Complaint)--+ → Client’s share: --+

Billing & Service Agreement

Service Agreement Summary

Service ProviderSeedXchange, LLC / CreditWithKen
ClientNew Client
Service TypeCredit Repair & Financial Restoration Services
Engagement Date--
File ReferenceCredit Restoration — CR-0000-0000
Total Service Fee--
Down Payment--
Remaining Balance--

Payment History

DateDescriptionAmountStatus
-- Down Payment — Engagement Fee -- PAID
Ongoing Remaining Balance — Service Fee -- OUTSTANDING

Case Outcome Projections

Best Case Scenario

--+
  • Full deposit return: -- (outstanding balance)
  • 2x bad faith penalty (CC §1950.5(l)): --
  • Fraud (Concealment) damages: compensatory + punitive
  • Conversion damages: value of withheld funds + interest
  • Constructive trust / equitable lien on property proceeds
  • Breach of fiduciary duty damages (broker --)
  • Fees, costs, and interest recovery
  • All 6 active disputes prevail — jury trial demanded

Worst Case Scenario

--
  • Demurrer sustained on weaker causes (Constructive Trust, Fiduciary Duty)
  • Partial deposit return only (-- max without penalties)
  • Deductions partially upheld by court
  • Service issues delay proceedings against respondents
  • Fraud concealment claim insufficient evidence
  • Settlement below full recovery

Terms & Conditions

SEEDXCHANGE, LLC / CREDITWITHKEN LEGAL RESEARCH & DOCUMENT PREPARATION AGREEMENT

1. Scope of Services: SeedXchange, LLC (“Provider”) provides consumer law research, document preparation, and credit clean-up services. Provider does NOT provide legal advice, legal representation, or act as an attorney. Provider is NOT a law firm.

2. No Attorney-Client Relationship: Use of Provider’s services does not create an attorney-client relationship. If legal representation is required, Client must retain a licensed attorney independently.

3. Service Fee: Total service fee is -- with a down payment of -- due at engagement. Remaining balance is payable per agreed schedule.

4. No Guarantee of Outcomes: Provider makes no guarantees regarding case outcomes, settlement amounts, or recovery. Results depend on facts, opposing party behavior, and court determinations.

5. Confidentiality: All communications, documents, and case information are treated as confidential. Provider will not disclose Client information to third parties except as required by law or as authorized by Client.

6. Client Obligations: Client agrees to provide truthful, complete information and documentation. Client acknowledges that incomplete or inaccurate information may adversely affect service quality.

7. Communication: All communications regarding this engagement will be routed through the HIRECAR Member Services Portal and/or via Twilio-powered messaging (SMS/email). Messages are sent from SeedXchange regarding the Credit Repair Agreement.

8. Intellectual Property: All research, templates, methodologies, and frameworks used by Provider are proprietary to SeedXchange, LLC / CreditWithKen, LLC / HIRECAR, LLC.

9. Governing Law: This agreement is governed by the laws of the State of California.

SMS Notification Settings

Client will receive SMS copies of all portal messages from SeedXchange regarding the Credit Repair Agreement.

SMS messages will appear as: “SeedXchange Credit Repair”

ASAP Auto Loan Approval Program CreditWithKen — Client Playbook

Client Playbook

Your complete guide to the ASAP Auto Loan Approval Program

Table of Contents

15 Sections

  • 1Welcome
  • 2Client Profile
  • 3Program Overview
  • 4Payment & Enrollment
  • 5Start Here — Onboarding
  • 6Secure Upload Vault
  • 7Program Dashboard
  • 8Score & Credit Tracking
  • 9Credit Sweep & Cleanup
  • 10Tradelines & AU
  • 11Counseling Sessions
  • 12Lender Readiness
  • 13Dealer & Deal Support
  • 14Messages & Q&A
  • 15Completion Packet
Section 1 of 15

Welcome & How to Use This Playbook

Welcome to the ASAP Auto Loan Approval Program by CreditWithKen. This playbook is your personal command center — everything you need to repair your credit, build lender-ready scores, and secure auto financing lives here.

How This Works

Each section of this playbook represents a stage in your journey. Work through them in order, or jump to any section using the sidebar. Your progress is tracked automatically.

What You'll Find Inside

  • Your Profile & Score — Current credit snapshot and CreditWithKen Score
  • Program Overview — What's included, timelines, and expectations
  • Onboarding Gate — Required documents and "clock start" checklist
  • Upload Vault — Secure document exchange portal
  • Progress Dashboard — Real-time status on disputes, tradelines, and milestones
  • Credit Tracking — Score history and credit file changes
  • Cleanup Plan — Dispute strategy and sweep timeline
  • Tradeline Coordination — AU accounts and seasoning schedule
  • Counseling Hub — Schedule and notes from your 3 coaching sessions
  • Lender Toolkit — Pre-qualification checklist and lender matching
  • Deal Support — Dealer negotiation prep and deal review
  • Messages & Decisions — Communication log and Q&A history
  • Completion Packet — Final deliverables and graduation certificate
Important

This playbook is confidential. Do not share access credentials or screenshots with third parties. Your data is encrypted and protected under our privacy policy.

Section 2 of 15

Client Profile / CreditWithKen Score

Your Credit Snapshot

FieldValue
Client Name--
Program EnrolledASAP Auto Loan Approval
Enrollment Date--
CreditWithKen ScorePending Assessment
Starting FICO (Equifax)--
Starting FICO (Experian)--
Starting FICO (TransUnion)--
Target Score Range680 – 720+
Estimated Timeline90 – 120 days
CreditWithKen Score

Your CWK Score is a proprietary lender-readiness rating that factors in FICO scores, credit mix, payment history patterns, utilization, and auto-specific lending criteria. It's updated as your file improves.

Section 3 of 15

Program Overview

ASAP Auto Loan Approval Program

DetailDescription
SKU / Program CodeASAP-ALR-2026
Duration90 – 120 days (active phase)
Support Window180 days from enrollment
Counseling Sessions3 × 30-minute sessions included
Dispute RoundsUp to 4 rounds across all 3 bureaus
Tradeline CoordinationAU strategy + placement guidance
Lender MatchingPre-qualification with partner lenders

Deliverables

  • Full 3-bureau credit analysis and dispute strategy
  • Up to 4 dispute rounds with all credit bureaus and furnishers
  • Authorized User (AU) tradeline strategy and coordination
  • 3 one-on-one credit counseling sessions (30 min each)
  • Lender readiness assessment and pre-qualification packet
  • Dealer negotiation prep materials
  • Completion certificate and final credit summary

Expectations

Your Commitment

Success requires your active participation. Respond to requests within 48 hours, provide accurate documentation, avoid new hard inquiries, keep credit utilization under 30%, and attend all scheduled counseling sessions.

Section 4 of 15

Payment & Enrollment

Payment Schedule

PaymentAmountDueStatus
Enrollment Fee--At signupPending
First Work Fee--After onboardingPending
Second Work Fee--At midpointPending
Final Fee--At completionPending
Payment Policy

All payments are processed securely. Work does not begin until the enrollment fee is received and onboarding documents are submitted. Refund policy details are included in your service agreement.

Section 5 of 15

Start Here — Onboarding Gate

Clock Start

Your program timeline does NOT begin until all onboarding items are complete. The faster you submit, the faster we start working.

Onboarding Checklist

  • OKService agreement signed
  • OKEnrollment fee received
  • Government-issued ID (front + back)
  • Proof of address (utility bill, bank statement)
  • Social Security card or W-2
  • Most recent credit reports (all 3 bureaus)
  • Credit monitoring login credentials
  • List of accounts to dispute (if known)
  • Initial counseling session scheduled

Onboarding Progress

2 of 9 items complete

Section 6 of 15

Secure Upload Vault

Use the vault below to securely upload required documents. All files are encrypted in transit and at rest. Accepted formats: PDF, JPG, PNG (max 25MB per file).

Upload Documents

UPLOAD

Drag & drop files here

or click to browse — PDF, JPG, PNG (max 25MB)

Uploaded Documents

DocumentDateStatus
No documents uploaded yet
Section 7 of 15

Program Dashboard — Progress & Status

Overall Progress

Phase: Onboarding

Milestone Tracker

MilestoneTargetStatus
Onboarding CompleteWeek 1In Progress
First Dispute Round SentWeek 2Pending
First Results ReceivedWeek 6 – 8Pending
Second Dispute RoundWeek 8Pending
Tradeline Strategy ActiveWeek 4Pending

Score & Credit File Tracking

Score History

DateEquifaxExperianTransUnionNotes
Baseline------Initial pull

Scores are updated after each dispute cycle and significant credit events.

Credit File Changes

DateBureauChangeImpact
No changes recorded yet
Section 9 of 15

Credit Sweep & Cleanup Plan

Dispute Strategy

Your cleanup plan targets negative items across all three bureaus. Each round addresses specific accounts based on priority, likelihood of removal, and scoring impact.

Dispute Rounds

RoundSentAccounts TargetedResultsStatus
Round 1------Pending
Round 2------Pending
Round 3------Pending
Round 4------Pending

Items Under Dispute

Account / CreditorBureau(s)TypeBalanceStatus
Dispute targets will be identified after credit analysis
Section 10 of 15

Tradelines & Authorized User (AU) Coordination

Authorized User tradelines can significantly boost your credit scores by adding seasoned, positive payment history to your file. We coordinate AU placements based on your specific needs.

AU Strategy

TradelineCredit LimitAgeBureau(s)AddedStatus
AU strategy will be developed after initial credit analysis
Seasoning Timeline

Most AU tradelines take 1 – 2 billing cycles (30 – 60 days) to report to bureaus after being added. We time placements to align with your dispute results for maximum impact.

Section 11 of 15

Counseling Sessions Hub

Your program includes 3 one-on-one counseling sessions (30 minutes each) with your CreditWithKen advisor. These sessions cover credit strategy, financial habits, lender preparation, and any questions about your case.

1

Session 1: Credit Analysis & Strategy Review

Review your credit reports, identify targets, discuss dispute strategy and timeline expectations.

Not Scheduled
2

Session 2: Midpoint Check-In & AU Planning

Review dispute results, adjust strategy, plan tradeline placements and next steps.

Not Scheduled
3

Session 3: Lender Readiness & Deal Prep

Final score review, lender matching, dealer negotiation prep, and graduation walkthrough.

Not Scheduled
Section 12 of 15

Lender Readiness Toolkit

Pre-Qualification Checklist

  • Credit scores at or above 680 target
  • No new negative items in past 6 months
  • Credit utilization under 30%
  • At least 3 active positive tradelines
  • Stable income documentation ready
  • Down payment funds verified
  • Debt-to-income ratio under 45%
  • No open disputes at time of application

Lender Matching

LenderTypeMin ScoreRate RangeMatch Status
Lender matching begins after credit repair phase is complete
Section 13 of 15

Dealer & Deal Support

Negotiation Prep

Before stepping into a dealership, you'll be armed with the knowledge and documents to negotiate from a position of strength. We review every deal before you sign.

Deal Review Checklist

  • Pre-approved financing secured
  • Target vehicle identified (year, make, model)
  • Fair market value researched (KBB / Edmunds)
  • Trade-in value assessed (if applicable)
  • Monthly payment budget confirmed
  • Dealer markup / add-ons review
  • Final deal terms reviewed by CreditWithKen
Pro Tip

Never tell the dealer your monthly payment budget first. Negotiate the total price of the vehicle, then discuss financing terms separately. Send us the deal sheet before signing — we'll review it within 24 hours.

Section 14 of 15

Messages, Q&A, and Decisions Log

This is your communication record with CreditWithKen. All messages, questions, and decisions made during the program are logged here for reference.

Recent Messages

DateFromSubjectStatus
No messages yet — communication will appear here as your program progresses

Decisions Log

DateDecisionDetails
No decisions logged yet
Section 15 of 15

Completion Packet

Program Graduation

Upon successful completion of the ASAP Auto Loan Approval Program, you'll receive your Completion Packet containing all deliverables, final credit summary, and graduation certificate.

Final Deliverables

  • Final 3-bureau credit report summary
  • Score improvement documentation
  • Dispute results summary (all rounds)
  • Tradeline activity report
  • Lender pre-qualification letters
  • Counseling session notes
  • Credit maintenance guide (post-program)
  • CreditWithKen Completion Certificate

Post-Program Support

After graduation, you'll have 30 days of follow-up support for any questions about maintaining your credit, managing your new auto loan, or dealing with any remaining credit file concerns.

End of Playbook

CreditWithKen — ASAP Auto Loan Approval Program

Hire_bot Assistant
Hi there, I'm your CreditWithKen case assistant. I can help you find documents, check deadlines, or navigate your case. What do you need?
